paper_title: Tolerability and Safety of Sublingual Immunotherapy in Patients with Tree Pollen Allergy in Daily Practice—An Open, Prospective, Non-Interventional Study

methods.description: Process for recruiting and enrolling patients for a study on sublingual immunotherapy (SLIT) for tree pollen allergies.

steps.description: Patients were recruited between autumn 2018 and December 2019 by allergologists.
Patients were enrolled in the study after the decision for treatment with birch or tree pollen sublingual immunotherapy (SLIT) had already been made.

methods.description: Procedure for conducting study visits and collecting data from patients undergoing SLIT.

steps.description: Overall, 5 visits at the physician's office were planned: a screening visit (V1), an administration visit (V2), two maintenance visits (V3, V4), and an end-of-study visit (V5).
The investigators completed an electronic case report form (eCRF) for each patient during every visit.
steps.searchable_summary: complete eCRF for each patient visit

steps.description: At the first visit, V1, the investigators checked the in-and exclusion criteria and documented demographic data, medical history, concomitant diseases, and/or medication usage.
steps.searchable_summary: check criteria and document patient data at V1

steps.description: During the second visit, V2, the SLIT with SBPE or STPE was initiated and the date and time of the first use as well as early local or systemic reactions were documented.
steps.searchable_summary: initiate SLIT and document reactions at V2

steps.description: During the following two maintenance visits, V3 and V4, treatment compliance and occurrence of AEs were checked and documented by the investigators.
steps.searchable_summary: check and document compliance and AEs at V3 and V4

steps.description: During the last visit, V5, patients were asked about their treatment satisfaction, and AE data were collected.
steps.searchable_summary: collect treatment satisfaction and AE data at V5

methods.description: Procedure for administering sublingual immunotherapy (SLIT) to patients with tree pollen allergies.

steps.description: Patients started the treatment with one drop and added one drop each consecutive day until the maintenance dose of five drops per day was reached.
steps.searchable_summary: administer SLIT drops to reach maintenance dose

steps.description: Drops were held underneath the tongue for 2-3 min before swallowing.
steps.searchable_summary: hold SLIT drops under tongue before swallowing

steps.description: In case of local or systemic reactions, dose adjustments according to the SPC were performed.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust SLIT dose based on reactions

methods.description: Monitoring and documenting patient adherence and compliance during the SLIT treatment.

steps.description: The treatment compliance during SBPE or STPE treatment in this NIS is shown in Figure 1.
Patients were asked whether they took the drops daily during the treatment period.
steps.searchable_summary: survey patients on daily SLIT drop intake
